It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|ja|天神神社 (瑞穂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}
Moto-Ise Shrines are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ([[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]).
* {{Infobox religious building | name = '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) | image = File:Tenjin jinja2007-1.jpg | caption = Shrine approach | location = {{ill|Ikura, Mizuho|de|Ikura, Mizuho|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura, Mizuho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.422444|N|136.643722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] and [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] | shrine_rank = Former Village shrine | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare style]] | alternate_name= | festival = [[:en:March 11|March 11]] | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Mizuho|WD=Q782103}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]]
[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]], Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] searched for a site to enshrine the [[:en:Shintai|Goshintai]] of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], traveling from Sakata-no-miya in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] to the Ikura River in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]].
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from [[:en:Mieji Station|Mieji Station]] on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from [[:en:Hozumi Station|Hozumi Station]] on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”
==Footnotes== ===Notes=== {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
===References=== {{Reflist}}
==Further reading==
{{ill|Shinto Taijiten Shukusai-ban|de|Shinto-Taijiten Kurzfassung|ja|神道大辞典 縮刷版|zh|神道大辞典 缩刷版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Taijiten Shukusai-ban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chūō-Kōron-Shinsha, 1969) ISBN 4-653-01347-0
{{ill|Jinja Meikan|de|Jinja Meikan|ja|神社名鑑|zh|神社名鉴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinja Meikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Jinja Honchō, 1963)
{{ill|Ise no Jingū: Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Gyunkō no Subete|de|Ise no Jingu|ja|伊勢の神宮【ヤマトヒメノミコト御巡幸のすべて】|zh|伊势神宫：大和姬命巡幸全记录|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Jingū: Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Gyunkō no Subete|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Izumi Shoin, 1993) ISBN 4-87088-609-X

Moto-Ise Shrines are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ([[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]).
* {{Infobox religious building | name = '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) | image = File:Tenjin jinja2007-1.jpg | caption = Shrine approach | location = {{ill|Ikura, Mizuho|de|Ikura, Mizuho|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura, Mizuho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.422444|N|136.643722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] and [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] | shrine_rank = Former Village shrine | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare style]] | alternate_name= | festival = [[:en:March 11|March 11]] | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Mizuho|WD=Q782103}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]]
[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]], Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] searched for a site to enshrine the [[:en:Shintai|Goshintai]] of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], traveling from Sakata-no-miya in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] to the Ikura River in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]].
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from [[:en:Mieji Station|Mieji Station]] on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from [[:en:Hozumi Station|Hozumi Station]] on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”

==Deities==
[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]]
[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]], Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] searched for a site to enshrine the [[:en:Shintai|Goshintai]] of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], traveling from Sakata-no-miya in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] to the Ikura River in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]].
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from [[:en:Mieji Station|Mieji Station]] on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from [[:en:Hozumi Station|Hozumi Station]] on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”
==Footnotes== ===Notes=== {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
===References=== {{Reflist}}
==Further reading==

{{ill|Jinja Meikan|de|Jinja Meikan|ja|神社名鑑|zh|神社名鉴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinja Meikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Jinja Honchō, 1963)

The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ([[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]).
* {{Infobox religious building | name = '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) | image = File:Tenjin jinja2007-1.jpg | caption = Shrine approach | location = {{ill|Ikura, Mizuho|de|Ikura, Mizuho|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura, Mizuho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.422444|N|136.643722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] and [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] | shrine_rank = Former Village shrine | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare style]] | alternate_name= | festival = [[:en:March 11|March 11]] | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Mizuho|WD=Q782103}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]]
[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]], Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] searched for a site to enshrine the [[:en:Shintai|Goshintai]] of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], traveling from Sakata-no-miya in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] to the Ikura River in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]].
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from [[:en:Mieji Station|Mieji Station]] on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from [[:en:Hozumi Station|Hozumi Station]] on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”

Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]].
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from [[:en:Mieji Station|Mieji Station]] on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from [[:en:Hozumi Station|Hozumi Station]] on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]]
[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]], Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] searched for a site to enshrine the [[:en:Shintai|Goshintai]] of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], traveling from Sakata-no-miya in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] to the Ikura River in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]].
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from [[:en:Mieji Station|Mieji Station]] on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from [[:en:Hozumi Station|Hozumi Station]] on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”

Approximately 30-minute walk from [[:en:Mieji Station|Mieji Station]] on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from [[:en:Hozumi Station|Hozumi Station]] on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”

It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|ja|天神神社 (瑞穂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}
Moto-Ise Shrines are [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ([[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]).
* {{Infobox religious building | name = '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) | image = File:Tenjin jinja2007-1.jpg | caption = Shrine approach | location = {{ill|Ikura, Mizuho|de|Ikura, Mizuho|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura, Mizuho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.422444|N|136.643722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] and [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] | shrine_rank = Former Village shrine | founded = Unknown | architecture = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare style]] | alternate_name= | festival = [[:en:March 11|March 11]] | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Mizuho|WD=Q782103}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
[[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]]
[[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]] [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Suinin]], Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] searched for a site to enshrine the [[:en:Shintai|Goshintai]] of [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu]], traveling from Sakata-no-miya in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] to the Ikura River in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]]. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty [[:en:Magatama|magatama]] made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]].
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Mizuho, Gifu|Mizuho]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from [[:en:Mieji Station|Mieji Station]] on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from [[:en:Hozumi Station|Hozumi Station]] on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”

It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|ja|天神神社 (瑞穂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}
Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).
* {{Infobox religious building | name = '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) | image = File:Tenjin jinja2007-1.jpg | caption = Shrine approach | location = {{ill|Ikura, Mizuho|de|Ikura, Mizuho|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura, Mizuho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.422444|N|136.643722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} | shrine_rank = Former Village shrine | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare style}} | alternate_name= | festival = {{ill|March 11|en|March 11|WD=Q2398|lt=March 11}} | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Mizuho|WD=Q782103}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Suinin}}, Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} searched for a site to enshrine the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai}} of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu}}, traveling from Sakata-no-miya in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Omi Province}} to the Ikura River in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}} made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=hatamoto}} class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}.
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from {{ill|Mieji Station|en|Mieji Station|WD=Q6843706|lt=Mieji Station}} on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from {{ill|Hozumi Station|en|Hozumi Station|WD=Q871149|lt=Hozumi Station}} on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”
==Footnotes== ===Notes=== {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
===References=== {{Reflist}}
==Further reading==
{{ill|Shinto Taijiten Shukusai-ban|de|Shinto-Taijiten Kurzfassung|ja|神道大辞典 縮刷版|zh|神道大辞典 缩刷版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Taijiten Shukusai-ban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chūō-Kōron-Shinsha, 1969) ISBN 4-653-01347-0
{{ill|Jinja Meikan|de|Jinja Meikan|ja|神社名鑑|zh|神社名鉴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinja Meikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Jinja Honchō, 1963)
{{ill|Ise no Jingū: Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Gyunkō no Subete|de|Ise no Jingu|ja|伊勢の神宮【ヤマトヒメノミコト御巡幸のすべて】|zh|伊势神宫：大和姬命巡幸全记录|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Jingū: Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Gyunkō no Subete|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Izumi Shoin, 1993) ISBN 4-87088-609-X

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|ja|天神神社 (瑞穂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}
Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).
* {{Infobox religious building | name = '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) | image = File:Tenjin jinja2007-1.jpg | caption = Shrine approach | location = {{ill|Ikura, Mizuho|de|Ikura, Mizuho|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura, Mizuho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.422444|N|136.643722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} | shrine_rank = Former Village shrine | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare style}} | alternate_name= | festival = {{ill|March 11|en|March 11|WD=Q2398|lt=March 11}} | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Mizuho|WD=Q782103}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Suinin}}, Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} searched for a site to enshrine the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai}} of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu}}, traveling from Sakata-no-miya in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Omi Province}} to the Ikura River in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}} made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=hatamoto}} class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}.
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from {{ill|Mieji Station|en|Mieji Station|WD=Q6843706|lt=Mieji Station}} on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from {{ill|Hozumi Station|en|Hozumi Station|WD=Q871149|lt=Hozumi Station}} on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”
==Footnotes== ===Notes=== {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
===References=== {{Reflist}}
==Further reading==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11443289]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to {{ill|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|en|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|WD=Q13429672|lt=Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion}} {{afc comment|1=to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|ja|天神神社 (瑞穂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}
Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).
* {{Infobox religious building | name = '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) | image = File:Tenjin jinja2007-1.jpg | caption = Shrine approach | location = {{ill|Ikura, Mizuho|de|Ikura, Mizuho|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura, Mizuho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.422444|N|136.643722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} | shrine_rank = Former Village shrine | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare style}} | alternate_name= | festival = {{ill|March 11|en|March 11|WD=Q2398|lt=March 11}} | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Mizuho|WD=Q782103}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Suinin}}, Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} searched for a site to enshrine the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai}} of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu}}, traveling from Sakata-no-miya in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Omi Province}} to the Ikura River in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}} made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=hatamoto}} class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}.
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from {{ill|Mieji Station|en|Mieji Station|WD=Q6843706|lt=Mieji Station}} on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from {{ill|Hozumi Station|en|Hozumi Station|WD=Q871149|lt=Hozumi Station}} on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”
==Footnotes== ===Notes=== {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
{{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|ja|天神神社 (瑞穂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}
Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).
* {{Infobox religious building | name = '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) | image = File:Tenjin jinja2007-1.jpg | caption = Shrine approach | location = {{ill|Ikura, Mizuho|de|Ikura, Mizuho|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura, Mizuho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.422444|N|136.643722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} | shrine_rank = Former Village shrine | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare style}} | alternate_name= | festival = {{ill|March 11|en|March 11|WD=Q2398|lt=March 11}} | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Mizuho|WD=Q782103}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Suinin}}, Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} searched for a site to enshrine the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai}} of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu}}, traveling from Sakata-no-miya in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Omi Province}} to the Ikura River in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}} made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=hatamoto}} class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}.
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from {{ill|Mieji Station|en|Mieji Station|WD=Q6843706|lt=Mieji Station}} on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from {{ill|Hozumi Station|en|Hozumi Station|WD=Q871149|lt=Hozumi Station}} on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”
==Footnotes== ===Notes=== {{Reflist|group="注釈"}}
===References=== {{Reflist}}
==Further reading==
{{ill|Shinto Taijiten Shukusai-ban|de|Shinto-Taijiten Kurzfassung|ja|神道大辞典 縮刷版|zh|神道大辞典 缩刷版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Taijiten Shukusai-ban|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Chūō-Kōron-Shinsha, 1969) ISBN 4-653-01347-0
{{ill|Jinja Meikan|de|Jinja Meikan|ja|神社名鑑|zh|神社名鉴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinja Meikan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Association of Shinto Shrines, 1963)
{{ill|Ise no Jingū: Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Gyunkō no Subete|de|Ise no Jingu|ja|伊勢の神宮【ヤマトヒメノミコト御巡幸のすべて】|zh|伊势神宫：大和姬命巡幸全记录|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Jingū: Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Gyunkō no Subete|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Izumi Shoin, 1993) ISBN 4-87088-609-X

{{ill|Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|ja|天神神社 (瑞穂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin Shrine (Mizuhoshi City)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}}
Moto-Ise Shrines are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}}).
* {{Infobox religious building | name = '''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) | image = File:Tenjin jinja2007-1.jpg | caption = Shrine approach | location = {{ill|Ikura, Mizuho|de|Ikura, Mizuho|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura, Mizuho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.422444|N|136.643722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} | shrine_rank = Former Village shrine | founded = Unknown | architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare style}} | alternate_name= | festival = {{ill|March 11|en|March 11|WD=Q2398|lt=March 11}} | events = | prefecture= {{ill|Mizuho|WD=Q782103}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}} [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Suinin}}, Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}} searched for a site to enshrine the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai}} of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu}}, traveling from Sakata-no-miya in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Omi Province}} to the Ikura River in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}}. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama}} made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=hatamoto}} class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}.
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from {{ill|Mieji Station|en|Mieji Station|WD=Q6843706|lt=Mieji Station}} on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from {{ill|Hozumi Station|en|Hozumi Station|WD=Q871149|lt=Hozumi Station}} on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”

===References=== {{Reflist}}
==Further reading==

'''Tenjin Shrine''' (天神神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Ikura, Mizuho, Gifu Prefecture, Japan. The shrine's former rank was a Village Shrine. It is believed to be the site of the Ikuragawa-no-miya ({{ill|Ikuragawa-no-miya|de|Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja|伊久良河宮|zh|伊久良河宫|lt=Ikura River Palace|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikuragawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) where Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} stayed, making it one of the Moto Ise Shrines.
==Deities==
{{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi}} [[ja:天神神社 (瑞穂市)]]
==History== According to the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|de|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|zh|倭姬命世记|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, in the 10th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} searched for a site to enshrine the {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Goshintai|1=Shintai}} of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu|1=Amaterasu}}, traveling from Sakata-no-miya in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Omi Province|1=Ōmi Province}} to the Ikura River in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. Legend holds that a palace was established here as the Ikuragawa-no-miya, and the stone known as the {{ill|Mifunashiro-ishi|de|Mifunashiro-ishi|ja|御船代石|zh|御船代石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifunashiro-ishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the right of the main hall marks where her portable shrine was placed.Present-day Sakata Shinmei Shrine in Uga, Maibara, Shiga Prefecture: Sakata-no-miya reference. After four years, she departed to other sites along the river, eventually reaching the Nakashima-no-miya in present-day Ichinomiya.Potential sites include Sakemi Shrine and Sakate Shrine in present-day Ichinomiya.
Prior to the construction of a permanent shrine, the area was considered sacred ground, with rituals conducted around the Mifunashiro-ishi serving as an yorishiro. Archaeological finds around the stone include six {{ill|divine beast mirrors|de|Göttliche Tier-Spiegel|ja|神獣文鏡|zh|神兽文镜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine beast mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and over thirty {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}} made of jade, indicating ancient ceremonial activity.
During the Edo period, the Aoki family of the {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|WD=Q572662|lt=hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}} class patronized the shrine. The current main hall dates from a reconstruction in the Genroku era (1688–1704). Known previously as Ikuragawa-no-miya Tenjingū or Amatsujin Shrine, it was renamed Tenjin Shrine and designated a Village Shrine in 1873.
==Subsidiary Shrines== Two smaller shrines are located near the Mifunashiro-ishi, enshrining branch spirits of Ise Grand Shrine and Princess {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}.
==Cultural Properties==
Main hall of Tenjin Shrine (city-designated cultural property){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=2708&shrname=%E2%98%85%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85|title=Tenjin Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture Shrine Agency|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Site of Ikuragawa-no-miya (city-designated historic site){{Cite web|和書|date=2013-02-04|url=http://www.city.mizuho.lg.jp/mizuho/gaiyou/shizen/p-5679.html|title=Parks and Historic Sites|publisher=City of Mizuho|accessdate=2013-04-23}}
Tabunoki tree (city-designated natural monument)
==Location==
{{ill|Ikura|de|Ikura|ja|居倉|zh|居仓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mizuho, Gifu|en|Mizuho, Gifu|WD=Q782103|lt=Mizuho|1=Mizuho, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} Surrounded by narrow winding roads; landmarks include Ikura Community Center and Nentoku-ji Temple.
==Access==
Approximately 30-minute walk from {{ill|Mieji Station|en|Mieji Station|WD=Q6843706|lt=Mieji Station|1=Mieji Station}} on the Tarumi Railway.
Take the Mizuho City community bus (Mizuho Bus) from {{ill|Hozumi Station|en|Hozumi Station|WD=Q871149|lt=Hozumi Station|1=Hozumi Station}} on the Tōkaidō Main Line and alight at Ikura Community Center.
From the intersection of Gifu Prefectural Route 92 (Gifu–Sunami–Ōno Line) at Tanokami Akamiya, travel north approximately 1.5 km.
==Other== To the west stands the original tree of the Fuyu persimmon, one of the main varieties cultivated in the area, marked by a monument inscribed “Birthplace of the Fuyu Persimmon.”
